description: Fix duplicate bot messages: ensure each Slack event_id is processed exactly once, even under backlog replay or concurrent delivery

userStories.title: Deduplicate Slack events by event_id at the relay level using in-memory cache
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive exactly one bot response per message I send, regardless of Slack retries or backlog delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an in-memory Set (or LRU cache) in the relay to track processed event_ids
Before processing any Slack event, check if event_id is already in the set — if so, return 200 immediately without processing
Add the event_id to the set after accepting it for processing (before fire-and-forget, not after)
The cache should hold event_ids for at least 10 minutes (TTL) to cover Slack's retry window
Use a simple Map<string, number> where value is the timestamp of when it was added, evict entries older than 10 minutes
This deduplication is in ADDITION to the existing X-Slack-Retry-Num check (keep both)
Remove the 30-second stale event check added in PR #26 — it is too aggressive and drops legitimate events from users who message slowly. The event_id dedup is the correct solution.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: apps/slack-relay/src/app-factory.ts. The processedEventIds cache should be created inside buildSlackRelayApp() so it is per-app-instance (not global). Use a Map<string, number> where the value is Date.now() at insertion time. Before processing, check if event_id exists in map. If yes, log info and return 200. If no, add to map then proceed. Periodically evict old entries using setInterval every 5 minutes inside the app factory. The event_id comes from body.event_id (envelope.event_id). This handles BOTH: (1) Slack retry of same event (X-Slack-Retry-Num), (2) Slack backlog delivery of events we already processed.

userStories.title: Add tests for event_id deduplication
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that verify the relay processes each event_id exactly once.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: same event_id sent twice → only one call to processSlackEventWithConfig (or equivalent)
Test: different event_ids → both processed
Test: event_id dedup works across concurrent requests (fire-and-forget pattern)
Test: X-Slack-Retry-Num header still drops retries (existing behavior preserved)
All existing relay tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Add tests to apps/slack-relay/src/verify-signature.test.ts or create a new test file apps/slack-relay/src/dedup.test.ts. Use buildSlackRelayApp + app.inject() pattern. Send POST /slack/events twice with the same event_id and valid signature. Assert that the mock fetchFn (or processSlackEvent spy) was called only once.
